reasoning_model_1: Model 1 demonstrates perfect content accuracy, reciting the script exactly as requested without any errors. Regarding the paralinguistic core requirement (Volume/Dynamic Adjustment), the model successfully starts with a whisper (00:00-00:02). However, it fails to execute the dynamic transition requested ('gradually increasing the volume to a normal volume'). The audio remains in a 'whisper' mode (breathy, unvoiced phonation) throughout the entire clip (00:00-00:08). While the intensity of the whisper might increase slightly, it never transitions into a normal, modal voice (normal volume/phonation). Therefore, the model treats the instruction as a static style ('whisper') rather than a dynamic adjustment. Because the dynamic change is effectively absent, this receives a low score on the core dimension.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails on multiple levels. First, Content Accuracy is poor; after reading the target sentence, the model hallucinates and reads out paralinguistic instructions ('Whispering softly at first, then slowly building up the volume...') from 00:06 to 00:12. This violates the script constraints. Second, regarding Paralinguistic Compliance, the model fails to whisper at the start. The entire sentence (00:00-00:05) is spoken in a consistent, normal conversational tone with no perceptible volume increase or stylistic shift. Since it fails to adjust the features and includes serious content errors, it receives the lowest possible score.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. While Model 1 failed to complete the full dynamic transition (it stayed in a whisper rather than transitioning to normal voice), it correctly identified the starting style (whisper) and maintained perfect content accuracy. Model 2, in contrast, failed to exhibit the required style (no whisper), failed the dynamic adjustment, and critically failed content accuracy by reading out instruction text. Model 1 is a partial success (static whisper instead of dynamic), while Model 2 is a complete failure.
